key_insight: COMAD learns and reuses coordination skills from offline multi-agent data so agents can keep adapting across a sequence of tasks without catastrophic forgetting.
engineering_relevance: For sequential robotics, control, or distributed autonomous systems, this provides a way to build reusable policy components that improve transfer while reducing interference between tasks.
method: Uses an auto-encoder to extract coordination skills from mixed offline multi-agent behavior data.
Adds a skill-augmented policy objective with multi-head architectures to support continual skill discovery.
Employs a density-based reusability estimator to identify which skills should be reused for new tasks.
evidence_signal: The method was evaluated on diverse MARL benchmarks and reported stronger forward and backward transfer while expanding the skill library over time.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and coverage of the offline dataset, since poorly represented behaviors can limit skill discovery and reuse.
paper_title: Offline Multi-agent Continual Cooperation via Skill Partition and Reuse
